author: zlipeng
abstract: End-to-end workflow skill for multi-phase plans. Plans are stored in an external Obsidian vault (PLANS_VAULT_DIR, default ~/Documents/Obsidian/Plans), one subfolder per project named by the repo basename, with Obsidian frontmatter (project/project_root/status/phase/tags) and a per-project _index.md — so local plans never pollute the code repo. Execution branches off the main development branch, then runs Phase-by-Phase with two hard gates: a pre-Phase re-planning gate that re-validates remaining steps against what earlier Phases actually produced, and a post-Phase review gate that runs the bundled reviewer prompt in an isolated context (sub-agent or fresh session) so the reviewer cannot inherit the implementer's reasoning. Each Phase ends in a single commit that contains only the code change — no plan number, Phase number, or plan-process metadata in the message — while checkbox/Reviewed/commit-hash updates are written to the plan in the vault. Once all Phases pass review, a Test Cases section is appended mapping every Acceptance Criterion to a runnable case. Includes runtime adapters for Claude Code (/code-review skill or Agent sub-agent), Codex CLI (/review or subprocess session), and a generic inline fallback. Plan documents follow a language-priority rule: explicit user request > conversation language > English fallback.
